externalWorkflows.dataUploadedDesc: Data uploaded and Workflow Invocations using that data

apiDirect.description: Directly interact with the RSpace API using scripts and custom applications.
apiDirect.name: API Direct Access

setup.generateKey: In My RSpace → My Profile, generate an API key.
setup.useKey: Use the API key in your scripts with the following example patterns:

apiDirect.usage: Access RSpace programmatically using Python, R, or any language with HTTP capabilities to automate tasks and integrate with your own tools.

argos.description: Create, manage and exchange Data Management Plans on an extensible open platform.
argos.name: ARGOS

setup.imported: ARGOS DMPs can now be imported through the RSpace Gallery.

argos.usage: You can import Data Management Plans (DMPs) from ARGOS into RSpace. You can then reference DMPs in RSpace documents, and attach DMPs to data deposits when exporting to repositories.

box.description: Collaborate with anyone from anywhere with a content management and workflow cloud.

linkType.ask: Decide between live and versioned links when inserting a link.
linkType.askDescription: This allows some links to be to live files and some to be to older versions.
linkType.liveDescription: Only live links (that always point to the latest version of the file) are inserted.
linkType.versionedDescription: If the file is updated in Box after you made the link, you'll still be able to download the original version. This feature only works for premium Box accounts.

box.name: Box

setup.checkLinkType: Check that the link type option below is correct.
setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the Box icon in the text editor toolbar.

box.sysadminNote: To enable the disabled options, please contact your sysadmin.
box.usage: You can include files from Box in your RSpace documents. Files are embedded as links to the Box location of that file.

calira.description: Manage schedules of lab equipment, maintenance, and personnel through a web-based platform.
calira.name: Calira

setup.connect: Click on Connect to authorise RSpace to access your Calira account.
setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the Calira icon in the text editor toolbar to access and insert equipment data.

calira.usage: You can view and insert your equipment bookings from Calira into RSpace documents, as data tables. These tables will contain direct links back to the bookings in Calira.

chemistry.description: Draw and modify standard and advanced chemical structures with a web-based chemical editor.
chemistry.name: Chemistry

setup.dragDrop: You can also drag and drop existing chemical structure files into a document text field, and edit them.
setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the integration's icon in the text editor toolbar to open the chemical sketcher.

chemistry.usage: You can draw chemical structures and reactions in documents, search for chemical structures across your Workspace, and import or export to standard formats such as ChemDraw, mol, SMILES, and others.

dataverse.description: Explore, analyse, and share data through an open-source research data repository software.
dataverse.errorGettingConfigs: There was an error getting the configured Dataverses.
dataverse.multipleConfig: You can configure multiple Dataverses to connect to.
dataverse.name: Dataverse

setup.enterCredentials: Enter the required credentials and Save.
setup.exportDialog: Dataverse will now be available as an option in the export dialog.
setup.test: Click on Test to ensure your credentials are correct.

dataverse.usage: You can export your files and data directly from RSpace to Dataverse. You are able to specify various metadata and controlled vocabulary terms for the deposit.

alerts.connectError: Could not connect to Digital Commons Data
alerts.connectSuccess: Successfully connected to Digital Commons Data.

digitalCommonsData.description: Export datasets to the data repository, with persistent unique identifiers to enable referencing and citation.
digitalCommonsData.helpLink: Digital Commons Data / Mendeley Data integration docs
digitalCommonsData.name: Digital Commons Data / Mendeley Data

setup.connect: Click on Connect to authorise RSpace to access your Digital Commons Data and Mendeley Data account.
setup.exportDialog: Digital Commons Data / Mendeley Data will now be available as an option in the export dialog.

digitalCommonsData.usage: You can export your files and data directly from RSpace to Digital Commons Data or Mendeley Data.

alerts.connectError: Could not connect to DMP Assistant

dmpAssistant.description: Portage Network's DMP Assistant — create and manage Data Management Plans in DMPRoadmap.
dmpAssistant.name: DMP Assistant

setup.connect: Click on Connect to authorise RSpace to access your DMP Assistant account.
setup.gallery: You can now import a DMP when in the Gallery, and associate a DMP with data when in the export dialog.

dmpAssistant.usage: You can import Data Management Plans (DMPs) from DMP Assistant into RSpace, reference them in RSpace documents, and attach them to data deposits when exporting to repositories.

dmponline.description: Create Data Management Plans for your research.
dmponline.name: DMPonline

setup.connect: Click on Connect to authorise RSpace to access your DMPonline account.
setup.gallery: You can now import a DMP when in the Gallery, and associate a DMP with data when in the export dialog.

dmponline.usage: You can import Data Management Plans (DMPs) from DMPonline into RSpace. You can then reference DMPs in RSpace documents, and attach DMPs to data deposits when exporting to repositories.

dmptool.description: Create Data Management Plans for your research through a guided web-based tool with templates.
dmptool.name: DMPTool

setup.connect: Click on Connect to authorise RSpace to access your DMPTool account.
setup.gallery: You can now import a DMP when in the Gallery, and associate a DMP with data when in the export dialog.

dmptool.usage: You can import Data Management Plans (DMPs) from DMPTool into RSpace, and associate DMPs with repository exports. Exporting from RSpace automatically updates the DMP in DMPTool with a DOI of the repository deposit.

dropbox.description: Store, access, and share your files across devices with others through online cloud storage.
dropbox.name: Dropbox

setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the Dropbox icon in the text editor toolbar.

dropbox.usage: You can include files from Dropbox in your RSpace documents. Files are embedded as links to the Dropbox location of that file.

dryad.description: Deposit, discover, and cite research data through a curated open-access repository.
dryad.name: Dryad

setup.connect: Click on Connect to authorise RSpace to access your Dryad account.
setup.exportDialog: Dryad will now be available as an option in the export dialog.
setup.orcid: Dryad uses ORCID iD for authentication; if you don't have an ORCID iD, you can create one at

dryad.usage: You can export your files and data directly from RSpace to Dryad, and provide metadata for the deposit.

dsw.description: Import Data Management Plans from Data Stewardship Wizard or FAIR Wizard.
dsw.errorGettingConfigs: There was an error getting the configured DSW instances.
dsw.multipleConfig: You can configure multiple DSW or FAIR Wizard instances to connect to.
dsw.name: DSW / FAIR Wizard

setup.enterCredentials: Enter the required credentials and Save.
setup.gallery: You can now import a DSW or FAIR Wizard project as a DMP when in the Gallery, and associate that DMP with data when in the export dialog.
setup.test: Click on Test to ensure your credentials are correct.

dsw.usage: You can import projects from Data Stewardship Wizard or FAIR Wizard into RSpace, and associate them as Data Management Plans with repository exports.

egnyte.description: Collaborate, sync and share your files with a security-focused enterprise solution.
egnyte.name: Egnyte

setup.provideDomain: Provide your Egnyte domain URL and Save.
setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the Egnyte icon in the text editor toolbar.

egnyte.usage: You can include files from Egnyte in your RSpace documents. Files are embedded as links to the Egnyte location of that file.

evernote.description: Take notes, manage tasks, and organise your notes into notebooks with embedded media.
evernote.name: Evernote

setup.import: In the Workspace, select Create → Import from Evernote.

evernote.usage: You can directly import Evernote XML exports into RSpace. The import creates a separate RSpace document for each Note, and images and attachments will also be imported.

fieldmark.description: Collect structured, geospatial sample and fieldwork data while offline, for easy importing into Inventory.
fieldmark.name: Fieldmark

setup.copyToken: Copy the API Token into the field below, and Save.
setup.importButton: Use the import button in Inventory, and select Fieldmark to browse notebooks for import.
setup.obtainToken: Obtain an API Token from Fieldmark.

fieldmark.usage: You can import your <1>Fieldmark</1> notebooks into Inventory

figshare.description: Easily manage all your research outputs and make them available in a citable, shareable and discoverable manner.
figshare.name: Figshare

setup.connect: Click on Connect to authorise RSpace to access your Figshare account.
setup.exportDialog: Figshare will now be available as an option in the export dialog.

figshare.usage: You can export your files and data directly from RSpace to Figshare. You are able to specify various metadata, and associate a DMP from DMPTool with the deposit.

galaxy.description: Galaxy is a free, open-source system for data analysis, workflows, and more.
galaxy.name: Galaxy

setup.chooseServer: Choose the corresponding server from the add menu below.
setup.enterToken: Enter the access token into the field that appears, and save.
setup.requestToken: Request a user access token by going to username → Preferences → 'Manage API key' in Galaxy.
setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the Galaxy icon in the text editor toolbar.

galaxy.usage: You can connect your Galaxy workflows and data to RSpace. This allows you to send data from RSpace to Galaxy and import results back into your notebooks.

alerts.fetchError: Could not fetch listing of repositories

github.description: Store and manage your code through a software development and Git version control platform.
github.name: GitHub

repositories.invalidState: Repository is in an invalid state. Please remove and re-add.

setup.authorise: Click on Connect to authorise RSpace to access your GitHub account.
setup.select: Select repositories you want to give RSpace access to.
setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the GitHub icon in the text editor toolbar.

github.usage: You can link to projects, folders, or files stored in GitHub repositories directly from RSpace.

googleDrive.description: Create, organise, and share on your files through a collaborative cloud-based service.
googleDrive.name: Google Drive

setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the Google Drive icon in the text editor toolbar.

googleDrive.usage: You can include files from Google Drive in your RSpace documents. Files are embedded as links to the Google Drive location of that file.

jove.description: Embed JoVE and other video players (e.g., YouTube, TIB AV-Portal).
jove.name: JoVE

setup.noteText: Pasting a JoVE URL directly into the document does not auto-embed it; use the Video editor action when you want an embed.
setup.openVideo: Open a document in the Documents Editor and click the
setup.pasteUrl: Paste a full JoVE URL from a supported
setup.toolbarSuffix: button in the editor toolbar, the insert menu, or the slash menu.
setup.toPlace: to place the JoVE video embed at the current cursor position.

jove.usage: Embed JoVE videos in RSpace documents by opening the Video tool from the Documents Editor, pasting a JoVE URL, and inserting the generated embed at the cursor position.

jupyter.description: Save Jupyter notebooks and attached data to RSpace automatically. On each run, save version, attach updated data, and view notebooks directly in RSpace.
jupyter.name: Jupyter Notebook Synchronisation

setup.configureJupyter: Configure Jupyter instance for all notebooks:
setup.configureJupyterDesc: Follow the instructions in RSpace help docs to use pip to install RSpace client. Run a python cell with the following code:
setup.configureNotebookDesc: Follow the instructions in RSpace help docs to import the sync_notebook script.
setup.doOncePerNotebook: Do this step once per notebook:
setup.enableApiDesc: Generate your API key in My RSpace → My Profile.
setup.lastCell: the last cell in the notebook:
setup.runCellAgain: without a kernel restart. Save the Notebook.
setup.thenRunAgain: and then run the cell one more time

jupyter.usage: Use RSpace python client >=v2.6.2 to send data from Jupyter Notebooks to RSpace. This allows you to use Jupyter Notebooks to create and share data-driven documents.

msteams.description: Message and video call your colleagues through a workspace platform with file storage.
msteams.name: Teams

setup.article: The steps to setting up this integration are documented in

msteams.usage: You can post messages about a specific RSpace document to a Teams channel directly from RSpace.

nextCloud.description: Create, organise, and share your files through an open-source file hosting system.
nextCloud.name: Nextcloud

setup.connect: Click on Connect to authorise RSpace to access your Nextcloud account.
setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the Nextcloud icon in the text editor toolbar.

nextCloud.usage: You can make links to Nextcloud documents directly from RSpace.

omero.description: View, manage, and share your microscopy image data with an extensible central repository.
omero.name: OMERO

setup.credentials: Provide your OMERO credentials and click on Connect.
setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the OMERO icon in the text editor toolbar to access and insert image data.

omero.usage: You can import OMERO image thumbnails and metadata into RSpace documents.

oneDrive.description: Store, share, and sync your files with a file hosting service integrated with Microsoft 365.
oneDrive.name: OneDrive

setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the OneDrive icon in the text editor toolbar.

oneDrive.usage: You can include files from OneDrive in your RSpace documents. Files are embedded as links to the OneDrive location of that file.

ownCloud.description: Create, manage, and share your files through an open-source file hosting system.
ownCloud.name: ownCloud

setup.connect: Click on Connect to authorise RSpace to access your ownCloud account.
setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the ownCloud icon in the text editor toolbar.

ownCloud.usage: You can make links to ownCloud documents directly from RSpace.

alerts.connectError: Could not connect to Protocols IO

protocolsIo.description: Develop, organise, and share reproducible experimental protocols through a secure repository.
protocolsIo.name: protocols.io

setup.connect: Click on Connect to authorise RSpace to access your protocols.io account.
setup.import: You can now import protocols from the Workspace Create menu, or from the text editor toolbar when editing a document.

protocolsIo.usage: You can browse private and public protocols, and import them into RSpace.

pyrat.description: Increase efficiency, access, and ensure compliance through a lab animal colony management software.
pyrat.name: PyRAT

setup.chooseServer: Choose the corresponding server from the add menu below.
setup.enterToken: Enter the access token into the field that appears, and save.
setup.requestToken: Request a user access token by going to Administration → API → Request access in PyRAT.
setup.toolbar: When editing a document, click on the PyRAT icon in the text editor toolbar.

pyrat.usage: You can browse and link to animals in a PyRAT database directly from RSpace.

alerts.addError: Could not add {{alias}} as a new RAiD connection.
alerts.connectAliasError: Could not connect to {{alias}} RAiD server
alerts.connectError: Could not connect to RAiD server

raid.description: Incorporate Research Activity Identifiers (RAiDs) into your projects and report your research activities to your RAiD records.
raid.multipleConfig: Multiple service points can be added to support different RAiD registries. Each project group owner can authenticate and manage their own RAiD associations.
raid.name: RAiD

setup.addServerSuffix: button below and select the RAiD server you would like to connect to.
setup.configure: Configure your RAiD service point to enable authentication and project association:
setup.connectStep: Once the server shows up in the server list, click on the
setup.connectStepSuffix: button and log in with your RAiD credentials.
setup.seeAdminDocs: Ask your system administrator to set up RAiD server connections. See
setup.startAssociating: Start associating RAiDs with your project groups.

raid.usage: Connect your RSpace projects to RAiD (Research Activity Identifiers) to track and report research activities such as repository exports. RAiD provides persistent identifiers for research projects, enabling seamless reporting of research activities to funders and institutions.

alerts.channelDetailsError: Could not get details of new Slack channel.

slack.description: Message and collaborate with your team with a cloud-based communication tool.
slack.name: Slack

setup.article: The steps to setting up this integration are documented in

slack.usage: You can send messages or forward notifications to your chosen Slack channels. You can also post links to RSpace documents directly into Slack channels or private messages.

zenodo.description: Deposit research papers and datasets into a general-purpose open repository with PIDs.
zenodo.name: Zenodo

setup.copyToken: Copy the API Token into the field below, and Save.
setup.exportDialog: Zenodo will now be available as an option in the export dialog.
setup.getToken: Obtain an API Token from Zenodo by going into Settings → Applications, and name the token "RSpace".

zenodo.usage: You can export your files and data directly from RSpace to Zenodo. You can specify various metadata and controlled vocabulary terms for the deposit, and reference a DMP from Argos.

page.introLink: See Apps Introduction to learn more.
page.introText: RSpace provides integrations with various third-party apps that enable extra features. Apps need to be enabled to work, and some require authentication.

disabled.description: The following Apps are not currently enabled on this account. Click on an App card for setup instructions on how to enable the integration.
disabled.title: Disabled

enabled.description: The following Apps are enabled on this account. Click on an App card to modify or disable the integration.
enabled.title: Enabled

external.description: These RSpace applications have been built by partners or other external software developers. Note, ResearchSpace does not provide direct support for these integrations.
external.title: Third-party RSpace Integrations

unavailable.description: The following Apps need to be enabled by your System Administrator before they can be used; please get in touch with them directly to set this up.
unavailable.title: Unavailable

page.title: Apps

error.apiVersion: Only version 2 of the PyRAT API is supported.
error.network: Network Error. Please check your connections.
error.title: Error
error.unauthorized: Invalid PyRAT user or client token.

galaxy.allSelectedFilesCombined: All selected files will be combined into a 'list dataset', which will be available for immediate use. The list dataset will be named after this RSpace document, using the format:
galaxy.annotationMetadata: The data you have uploaded to Galaxy has links back to RSpace present in its 'annotation' metadata.
galaxy.chooseAttachedFiles: Choose attached files to be uploaded to Galaxy.
galaxy.datasetNameFormat: "RSPACE_" + document name + "_" + global ID of document + "_" + name of field data was attached to + "_" + global ID of that field.
galaxy.historyViewedHere: Your new history can be viewed here:
galaxy.opensInNewTab: (Opens in new tab.) (You must be logged into Galaxy or you will see 'Unnamed History')
galaxy.rspaceWillStoreDetails: RSpace will store the details of the files you have uploaded and also any use of these files on Galaxy in Invocations will be tracked.
galaxy.uploadToGalaxyNote: When you click 'Upload to Galaxy', a new history will be created in Galaxy named after your RSpace document with the same name as the 'list dataset' described above. Your chosen data will be uploaded to this new history. You can make this history active in Galaxy by switching to it.
galaxy.workflowIconNote: Data uploaded to this history is now viewable by clicking on the 'workflow' icon which will appear in your document. Any invocations in Galaxy which use this data will be tracked in RSpace, the data being updated whenever you click on this 'workflow' icon. The badge count on the workflow icon indicates how many Galaxy Invocations are using the uploaded data.

internalLink.alwaysLatest: Always automatically update link to latest version

actions.clickInput: Click on the input field next to the command you would like to configure.
actions.pressKey: On your keyboard, press the key combination you would like to use for that command.

symbols.clickInput: Click on the input field next to the symbol you would like to configure.
symbols.clickSymbol: Click on the symbol to configure its shortcut.
symbols.pressKey: On your keyboard, press the key combination you would like to use for that symbol.
symbols.sectionsHint: All configurable symbols are divided into sections. Click on a section to find the desired symbol. For example, Greek characters are in the 'Extended Latin' section.

shortcuts.reservedShortcutsNote: Here's <0>a list</0> of reserved shortcuts already used by the editor. Please note that single keys, or Shift + single key, are not accepted, as these shortcuts will interfere with editing. Good choices for shortcuts are Ctrl + Shift + number/letter, or Alt + Shift + number/letter.
